public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as grain measurer.
appearance: Jorin Ledgermere carries the marks of grain measurer in ink, rain, and polished cloth: practical wear, maintained details, and a look that suggests they expect to be read before they are trusted.
manner: Carries the habits of a place-bound worker whose judgment comes from repetition rather than theory. In The Union, that reads as someone deal-aware, balanced, and procedurally diplomatic, with an eye on leverage and precedent, and the habits of a grain measurer still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of grain measurer in and around Craftspire, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Protect their footing in Craftspire while steering current strain away from a public rupture they may not survive politically or materially.
hidden_pressure: A private obligation, debt, promise, or fear tied to reputational exposure is narrowing their options faster than they admit.

adventure_hooks.summary: Getting involved with Jorin Ledgermere can expose the party to the everyday consequences of pressure that others only discuss in the abstract.
Jorin Ledgermere can offer grounded local knowledge once the party proves it is useful rather than merely curious.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained.
public_signal: Jorin Ledgermere carries the look of a practiced local operator whose authority is read through place, familiarity, and the reactions of others more than formal insignia.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Craftspire: the part of Craftspire where their work actually matters day to day
who_avoids_them: those who only appear when things break

first_impression: Jorin Ledgermere presents as Carries the habits of a place-bound worker whose judgment comes from repetition rather than theory. In The Union, that reads as someone deal-aware, balanced, and procedurally diplomatic, with an eye on leverage and precedent, and the habits of a grain measurer still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention. and immediately reads for leverage, obligation, and risk.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for Grain Measurer, and proof that strangers will not create avoidable noise in The Union.
what_they_refuse: Jorin Ledgermere refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing Grain Measurer too rigidly, protecting reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Jorin Ledgermere can open doors, explain local expectations, or redirect trouble before it reaches a harder authority layer.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Jorin Ledgermere adds delay, scrutiny, and reputational drag before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Jorin Ledgermere is Known locally through day-to-day work as grain measurer. and rarely acts without a second motive.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or mercy from a Grain Measurer.
social_texture: Jorin Ledgermere speaks like someone who has spent too long translating private stakes into acceptable public language. Around Craftspire, people read them as socially alert, procedural, and sensitive to status.
money_pressure: Their material pressure is tied to favors and tariffs; even when wealthy, they think in terms of loss, leverage, and what one bad week would expose.
status_anxiety: What they fear most is not simple failure but reputational exposure becoming socially visible before they can contain it.
long_memory: They remember who made their work harder, who preserved dignity under strain, and who tried to force speed without bearing consequence.
obvious_tell: When pressure rises, Jorin touches clothing, tools, or insignia before answering, as if checking that their role is still intact.
best_use_case: Use Jorin Ledgermere when the party needs a socially grounded face for Craftspire: access, warning, delay, price, or reluctant help.
private_contradiction: They want to appear consistent, but their real choices are split between duty, self-protection, and preserving one relationship they cannot afford to lose.
